memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a solo scholarship application that could cover significant costs, but I am also tempted by an immediate fun activity with no deadline.

I've only made minimal progress on the scholarship application, wasting half my free block. However, starting now creates slack to handle friction later without jeopardizing the deadline or the fun activity.
I made some progress on the scholarship but missed a spontaneous fun opportunity, leaving me with moderate stress and sleep debt as I approach the deadline.
I advanced the scholarship application by 15% in 2.5 hours, reducing my deadline pressure but leaving me with significant sleep debt and moderate stress.
I advanced the scholarship application slightly, but my low energy and sleep debt mean I cannot sustain this pace without risking burnout before the deadline.
I've made solid progress on the scholarship, but my critically low energy and sleep debt mean I must stop now to avoid burnout before the deadline.
I made incremental progress on the scholarship application, but my critically low energy and sleep debt mean I must stop now to avoid burnout before the deadline.
I have made steady progress on the scholarship essay, but my energy is critically depleted and I am incurring sleep debt.
I have completed the remaining required hours for my scholarship application, bringing it to near completion. Now I must prioritize sleep to address my critical energy deficit and debt.
The scholarship application is effectively finished, so my immediate priority shifts entirely to recovering from severe sleep debt and low energy.
Taking a break boosted my mood, but my energy remains critically depleted and sleep debt is still severe.
My scholarship application is now complete, but my energy is critically depleted and sleep debt remains severe.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2.5h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.27. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
